UNLAWFUL DETAINER MOTION 501, PLAINTIFFS BRETT STAFFORD, KOICHI MATSUDA AND BARBARA BRADSHAW'S MOTION FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. THE CLASS IS OVERBROAD AS DEFINED. INDIVIDUAL ISSUES AMONG THE CLASS MEMBERS PREDOMINATE AND CLASS TREATMENT WILL NOT PROVIDE A SUBSTANTIAL BENEFIT TO THE COURT. THE COMMUNITY OF INTEREST REQUIREMENT IS NOT MET FOR MOST CAUSES OF ACTION BECAUSE EACH TENANT'S RIGHT TO RECOVER DEPENDS ON FACTS PECULIAR TO THAT TENANT. PREVAILING PARTY PREPARE ORDER. JUDGE A JAMES ROBERTSON, CLERK K. LIU, NOT REPORTED.

The tentative ruling is adopted as follows: CROSS COMPLAINANTs KAMRAN ARDEBILCHI and JAHANGIR ARDEBILCHI Amended Motion For Leave To File Amended Cross-Complaint To Substitute True Names For Fictitious Names. is denied - Failed to sustain burden, defendants were known entities and provides no authority. CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-24-2012 Set For Trial On May-20-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion For Class Certification
Off Calendar Oct-15-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances - Atty. Mark Hooshmand of Hooshmand Law Group (415-318-5709) appearing for plaintiff Brett Stafford moving party.
Atty. Jonathan Lieberman of Archer Norris a professional law group (925-930-6600) appearing for defendant Kamran Ardebilchi opposing party.

Tentative ruling is adopted, PLAINTIFFs BRETT STAFFORD, KOICHI MATSUDA and BARBARA BRADSHAW's Motion For Class Certification is denied without prejudice. The class is overbroad as defined. Individual issues among the class members predominate and class treatment will not provide a substantial benefit to the Court. The community of interest requirement is not met for most causes of action because each tenant's right to recover depends on facts peculiar to that tenant. Prevailing party prepare order. Judge A James Robertson, Clerk K. Liu, not reported. (AJR/501)
